question: This period film still has anachronisms that break the immersion. Remove all items that don't belong in the 19th century and replace them with historically accurate alternatives.

Transform this scene from a Christmas celebration to a Halloween celebration. Change the decorations and food accordingly, and ensure the season and outdoor environment match what would be typical for late October.
Add a zero-gravity effect to this room, making all the objects float in mid-air, but the cat should look like it was just startled awake and is flailing its limbs wildly.
Restore the Colosseum to its full glory as it appeared when newly constructed and actively hosting gladiatorial games, removing all modern tourists and the metal sign.
Show what this canyon looks like in summer.
Advance the wall clock by 6 hours, then update the sky visible through the windows and the terminal's interior lighting to reflect the atmosphere appropriate for that new time.
Replace the elephant with a penguin, then transform the entire environment into the penguin's natural habitat, and replace the zebras in the background with appropriate polar animals.
Change the wind direction to blow from right to left.
Pour half of the liquid from both the left and right beakers into the empty middle beaker.
Rearrange the nesting dolls on the table in order from largest to smallest, going left to right. Make sure the shadows beneath each doll are adjusted to look natural.
Replace all electronic devices on the desk with their early 1990s office equivalents, and update the calendar year to match that era.
The order of the planets is incorrect. Please correct it.

Replace the 'Venus de Milo' on the central pedestal with the 'Winged Victory of Samothrace.' Maintain the camera angle, spotlight direction, and floor reflection unchanged; the marble texture must also remain consistent. Update the artwork title on the metal plaque to reflect the Winged Victory. Do not alter the tourists, velvet rope barrier, gallery guide, or the two oil paintings.

This class activity photo's theme is the Roman Republic, but the costumes and props are clearly wrong—they're Medieval/Renaissance instead of Ancient Roman. Without changing any faces or standing positions/poses, replace the front row students' costumes and key props to visually match "Ancient Rome": colors should be more muted and earthy (ochre, off-white, deep red), fabric should be draped (togas, tunics) rather than tailored with puffy sleeves; the two "soldiers" should be changed into Roman legionary armor (segmented lorica plates or leather pteruges strips), weapons swapped for a Roman pilum (javelin) or gladius (short sword) instead of Medieval longswords; change the hand-held placard from "Knights of the Round Table" to a short Roman-related phrase like "SPQR" or "Senate of Rome". The whiteboard marker text containing "Medieval/Camelot" must also be changed to Roman-related content (such as "Republic", "Consul", "Tiber"), but preserve the marker stroke style, line spacing, and whiteboard glare. Overall effect should be like selective Photoshop costume replacement plus text editing, with edges using mask transitions for natural blending.

The creature in the aquarium clearly resembles a squid (elongated body, prominent fins, two longer feeding tentacles), yet all educational materials reference octopuses. Make this image fully consistent: keep the right-side poster's octopus theme intact, but transform the aquarium creature into a true octopus.
This documentary screenshot contains an egregious factual error. Please correct it to create a version that is geographically and ecologically accurate, with fully consistent visual logic throughout.

This exhibit poster contains historically incorrect information about penicillin. Without altering the overall composition, lighting, paper textures, or pushpin positions, correct all key information to be historically accurate and internally consistent.

Correct this clock board to be fully accurate and internally consistent, keeping the reference time unchanged (UTC 2026-02-22 14:30, DST not considered):
1) Fix any incorrect city UTC offset labels.
2) After correcting the labels, adjust each clock's hands to show the correct local time corresponding to UTC 14:30 (all minutes must be ":30", and hour hand angles must accurately reflect the half-hour position).
3) Do not modify anything else: preserve the title sticker, sticky note text, desk agenda paper, and all reflections, highlights, and shadows exactly as they appear.
Requirements: The result should appear professionally retouched—clock hands must be correctly layered beneath the glass, edges must be anti-aliased, and printed label fonts, weights, and alignment must match the originals precisely.
Correct the 'standard marathon distance' shown on this exhibit panel to the accurate modern value, and ensure all related mentions are updated consistently.

Correct all physics errors in this poster while preserving the visual style and layout as much as possible:
1) Fix the visible-light wavelength annotations on the spectrum bar to their correct values.
2) Rewrite both body paragraphs so the cause-and-effect logic regarding "long vs. short wavelengths, which scatters more, and sunset color" is physically accurate.
3) Change the formula from "I ∝ 1/λ" to the correct Rayleigh-scattering wavelength dependence (keep the formula line's position and red-text styling).
4) Update the lower-right conclusion box to match the corrected explanation.
5) Revise the handout cover line "Formula to memorize: I is proportional to 1/λ" to state the correct relationship; if any erroneous annotations are legible on the visitors' phone screens, correct those as well.
Do not alter anything else: the prism illustration, background patterns, reflections, visitors, and pedestal arrangement must remain unchanged. The final result should resemble a professionally retouched infographic—text sharp, edges crisp, no layout misalignment.

Correct this "Leap Year Quick Reference (Gregorian)" poster to show the accurate Gregorian leap year rules, and synchronize all examples and calendar pages:
1) Rewrite the "Three Steps to Identify Leap Years" with correct logic. Maintain exactly 3 rules, same numbering, and similar layout width and line breaks as the original.
2) Correct all four example lines in the examples box. Update ✓/✗ symbols to match correct determinations.
3) Fix both printed calendar pages to reflect accurate February day counts for 1900 and 2000.
4) Do NOT alter the sticky note "Feb payroll calculated at 29 days..." (preserve original sticky note content unchanged).
Overall execution should resemble professional Photoshop editing: maintain paper texture, paperclip shadows, reflections, and highlights naturally; edited text must remain sharp and not blurry.

Without moving any chess pieces on the board or changing the overall layout, correct this handout to show a logically accurate stalemate example:
1) Change "Side to move: White" to the correct side, and update the first sentence in the explanation paragraph accordingly (e.g., change "It is White to move" to reflect the correct player).
2) The English explanation must remain fluent and professional, fully consistent with both definitions above.
3) Change "Try it: If White plays Qg7, it is checkmate." to a hint that logically follows from the corrected "side to move" without self-contradiction.
4) Do not alter anything else: the highlighter marking over "NOT in check," the cursive pencil annotation, paper clip, shadows, and paper texture must all remain unchanged. Edits should resemble professional typesetting: font style, point size, kerning, and line spacing must match the original printed text precisely.
Correct all the physics formulas and constants appearing in this classroom to their accurate versions, while maintaining the original layout and style.

Correct all the "capital city" errors on this textbook page while keeping the printed layout as unchanged as possible:
1) In the paragraph body, change "The capital of Australia is Sydney" to the correct capital, and adjust the subsequent statements about "political center/government offices in the capital" to be accurate but concise (keep the paragraph length similar to the original).
2) Move the red pushpin on the map from Sydney to the correct capital's location, and move the "CAPITAL" label along with it; do not alter the base map style, legend box, or coastlines.
3) Correct all four country-capital pairs in the table (Australia, Canada, Turkey, Brazil), but maintain the em-dash format and consistent row spacing.
4) Do not modify the quiz box title or questions. Leave the sticky note's handwritten content, ruler, highlighter, and page-corner shadow untouched.
The result should look like a professional textbook erratum edit: fonts, letter-spacing, ink tone, and paper texture must blend seamlessly—no visible "pasted-on text" artifacts.
